title: Fibonacci — Make It O(n)
description:
Compute the nth Fibonacci number where fib(0) = 0, fib(1) = 1, fib(n) = fib(n-1) + fib(n-2).

The naive recursive version is **O(2ⁿ)** — completely impractical past n=40. Use memoization OR iteration to make it **O(n)**.

**The point**: see how caching subproblem answers collapses exponential work into linear work. This is the foundational idea of dynamic programming.

examples.explanation: The naive recursive version takes ~2 million calls; memoized takes 30.

hints: Recursive fib(n) recomputes fib(n-2) twice, fib(n-3) three times, fib(n-4) five times — Fibonacci-many times!
Memoization: cache each fib(k) the first time. Subsequent calls return instantly.
Iteratively, you only need the last two values. Walk from 2 to n updating (a, b) = (b, a+b).

testCases.description: would hang the naive recursive version
